core_mechanism: synchronous (lock-in / phase-sensitive) detection: correlating a noisy input against a periodic reference modulation at a known carrier frequency and integrating to recover a weak coherent component, with the reference waveform shaped to be smooth/triangular so its spectrum is compact and side-lobe (spectral) leakage is suppressed
brief_reason: The abstract recovers weak signals buried in noise by modulating the probe at the signal frequency and demodulating, and replaces pulsed (square-wave) modulation with adiabatically-generated triangular modulation specifically to eliminate spectral leakage, which is the defining move of shaped-reference synchronous detection.
